evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Plaintiff commenced this action seeking damages for injuries he allegedly sustained as a result of a laparoscopic ventral hernia repair performed by Kenneth J. Eckhert, III, M.D., F.A.C.S.
evidence_index.summary: Plaintiff claims injury from laparoscopic ventral hernia repair by Dr. Eckhert.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Plaintiff asserted causes of action for medical malpractice and lack of informed consent.
evidence_index.summary: Plaintiff claims medical malpractice and lack of informed consent.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Plaintiff submitted the affirmation of an expert gastrointestinal surgeon, who averred that an open procedure would have been more appropriate in plaintiff's situation because, based on plaintiff's surgical history, it should have been expected that plaintiff had abdominal adhesions.
evidence_index.summary: Expert surgeon suggests open procedure was more appropriate due to expected adhesions.
evidence_index.speaker: Plaintiff's expert

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Plaintiff testified at his deposition that defendant never informed him of alternatives to the laparoscopic procedure and that plaintiff was not aware of any at the time that he agreed to have the surgery.
evidence_index.summary: Plaintiff claims he was not informed of alternatives to laparoscopic procedure.
evidence_index.speaker: Plaintiff

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: We reject defendants' contention that Supreme Court erred in denying that part of their motion with respect to the cause of action for lack of informed consent.
evidence_index.summary: Court rejects defendants' argument on lack of informed consent.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: We also reject defendants' contention that the court erred in denying that part of their motion with respect to plaintiff's medical malpractice cause of action.
evidence_index.summary: Court rejects defendants' argument on medical malpractice claim.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Plaintiff submitted the affirmation of his expert surgeon and the affirmation of an expert pathologist, both of which 'squarely oppose[d]' the affirmation of defendants' expert.
evidence_index.summary: Plaintiff's experts oppose defendants' expert, creating a 'battle of the experts'.
evidence_index.speaker: Plaintiff's experts

simulation.testable_reason: The court found in favor of the plaintiff on both medical malpractice and lack of informed consent claims, indicating substantive liability was determined.

past_medical_history: Surgical history with expected abdominal adhesions

requestables.name: Informed Consent Discussion
requestables.description: Discussion of surgical options and risks

reveal.result_summary: Plaintiff was not informed of alternatives to laparoscopic procedure
reveal.decision_impact: Patient was not aware of surgical alternatives

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with ventral hernia and surgical history of adhesions
decision_points.prompt: What surgical approach should be considered?

options.description: Proceed with laparoscopic ventral hernia repair
options.clinical_reasoning: Defendant chose laparoscopic approach despite expected adhesions

options.description: Consider open surgical approach due to adhesions
options.clinical_reasoning: Expert testimony suggested open procedure was more appropriate

expected_action_court.description: Open procedure should have been considered
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence indicated open procedure was more appropriate

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Based on expert testimony that open procedure was more appropriate
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Open procedure should have been considered due to adhesions

decision_points.prompt: What should be included in the informed consent discussion?

options.description: Inform patient of laparoscopic and open surgical options
options.clinical_reasoning: Patient should be aware of all surgical options and associated risks
Plaintiff was not informed of alternatives

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant did not inform patient of alternatives

expected_action_court.description: Patient should have been informed of all surgical options
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Informed consent requires disclosure of alternatives

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to inform patient of alternatives constitutes lack of informed consent
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Patient should have been informed of both laparoscopic and open options

timeline_phases.name: Preoperative Assessment
timeline_phases.description: Assessment and planning for ventral hernia repair

key_events.event: Decision to proceed with laparoscopic repair

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to consider open procedure
Lack of informed consent for not disclosing alternatives

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to consider open procedure and lack of informed consent
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Open procedure should have been considered

factual_timeline.event: Decision to proceed with laparoscopic repair
